steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank describes how Rivera-Santos's conservation approach can be used with other Amazona species. The justification is that A. vittata "corresponds in physiology, behavior, and ecology" to those species — meaning what worked once should work again.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — replicable: replicable means capable of being reproduced elsewhere. Because the species share physiology and behavior, the same method can be applied again to other species. ✓
  • Choice B — comprehensible: comprehensible means easy to understand. The passage is about whether the method can be applied elsewhere, not whether it is easy to understand. ✗
  • Choice C — observable: observable means able to be seen. Observing an approach is not the same as being able to use it on other species. ✗
  • Choice D — redeemable: redeemable implies the approach is flawed and needs rescuing, which contradicts the passage's positive framing. ✗
That is the correct answer.
